steps.title: Identify the causal reasoning
steps.content: The passage centers on one key claim: the settlements were culturally linked to Izapa because they shared the same structural configuration (same age, pyramid north, plaza south). This causal relationship—structural similarity as evidence for cultural connection—is what the passage emphasizes as its main idea.

steps.title: Match the reasoning to the choices
steps.content: Choice A directly states this causal logic: researchers inferred the settlements were related to Izapa based on structural similarity. The other three either reverse the causal relationship, focus on the discovery of the settlements rather than the inference about their cultural connection, or claim a comparative importance of ceremonies that the passage never mentions. That matches choice A — A is correct.
